title: CatBoost Explicit Weights Without Auto Class Weights
summary: Remove CatBoost auto_class_weights while preserving the explicit fold-specific balanced sample weights passed into CatBoost.
feature_strategy: Keep all engineered features and auxiliary rows unchanged. This hypothesis changes only CatBoost class-balancing semantics, not the feature space or the other models.
baseline_model_panel: Use the current CatBoost, XGBoost, and LightGBM ensemble pipeline, but configure CatBoost without auto_class_weights="Balanced" because sample_weight=tree_sw already encodes fold-specific class balance and auxiliary locality.
model_panel_rationale: The parent improved by passing tree_sw to CatBoost, but CatBoost still also applies automatic class weighting. This experiment tests whether explicit balanced sample weights alone are cleaner than double balancing.
validation_strategy: Use the same 5-fold StratifiedKFold, same OOF model panel, same fold-wise logit blend, and same fold-wise temp-offset calibration. Primary score is balanced accuracy on competition OOF rows.
materialization_hint: Start from response.py. In make_cat_model, remove auto_class_weights="Balanced" and leave the CatBoost fit call using sample_weight=tree_sw. Preserve XGBoost/LightGBM weighting, auxiliary weights, features, blend search, calibration, and output artifacts.
expected_signal: If CatBoost was overcompensating class imbalance, its standalone OOF score and/or blend usefulness should improve. If auto_class_weights adds a useful extra bias, CatBoost should weaken and the ensemble score should decline.
risk: The competition hint favored CatBoost auto_class_weights, so disabling it may hurt. This is explicitly a controlled alternative class-weighting test.
rationale:
Hypothesis 000033: Remove CatBoost auto_class_weights while preserving the explicit fold-specific balanced sample weights passed into CatBoost.
Parent pipeline score reference: 0.968002 OOF balanced accuracy.
This experiment is intentionally atomic so the score movement can be attributed to the named change.
implementation_hint: Start from response.py. In make_cat_model, remove auto_class_weights="Balanced" and leave the CatBoost fit call using sample_weight=tree_sw. Preserve XGBoost/LightGBM weighting, auxiliary weights, features, blend search, calibration, and output artifacts.
expected_effect: If CatBoost was overcompensating class imbalance, its standalone OOF score and/or blend usefulness should improve. If auto_class_weights adds a useful extra bias, CatBoost should weaken and the ensemble score should decline.

parent_cv_reference.note: Use this hypothesis run score, not the parent score, as the empirical result.
